title: The Power of Practice Over Passive Learning
subtitle: 停止空想，立刻行动的力量

paragraphs.en: Most people have a dream in life, a vision of who or what they'd like to be in the future. At a minimum, everyone has interests and values that determine what they want out of their lives. Even so, trying to set achievable goals that you'll work for over the course of many years can be extremely challenging. It can be hard to know where to even begin, and the things you hope to achieve may seem impossible.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数人一生都有一个梦想，一个关于未来想成为什么样的人或做成什么样的事的愿景。至少，每个人都有决定自己想要什么的兴趣和价值观。即便如此，设定多年为之奋斗的可实现目标仍极具挑战性。甚至很难知道从何开始，而你希望达成的目标似乎遥不可及。

paragraphs.en: But, if you're well prepared you may be able to set goals for your life that are just as fulfilling to work toward as to achieve. We all have goals that we want to achieve in our lives. These goals may include learning a new language, eating healthier and losing weight, becoming a better parent, saving more money, and so on. It can be easy to assume that the gap between where you are now and where you want to be in the future is caused by a lack of knowledge.
paragraphs.zh: 但如果你准备充分，或许能为自己设定一些努力过程和实现结果同样令人满足的人生目标。我们都有想要实现的目标，可能包括学习一门新语言、健康饮食和减肥、成为更好的父母、存更多钱等等。人们很容易认为，你当前所处位置与未来目标位置之间的差距是由知识匮乏造成的。

paragraphs.en: This is why we buy courses on how to start a business or how to lose weight fast or how to learn a new language in three months. We assume that if we knew about a better strategy, then we would get better results. We believe that a new result requires new knowledge. What I'm starting to realize, however, is that new knowledge does not necessarily drive new results. In fact, learning something new can actually be a waste of time if your goal is to make progress and not simply gain additional knowledge.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是我们购买“如何创业”、“如何快速减肥”或“如何在三个月内学会一门语言”课程的原因。我们以为，如果知道更好的策略，就能获得更好的结果。我们相信新结果需要新知识。然而我开始意识到，新知识未必能带来新结果。事实上，如果你的目标是取得进展而非仅仅增加知识，学习新东西有时反而是浪费时间。

paragraphs.en: It all comes down to the difference between learning and practicing. Thomas Sterner, in his book The Practicing Mind, explains the key difference between practicing and learning. He says: 'When we practice something, we are involved in the deliberate repetition of a process with the intention of reaching a specific goal.' Here the words deliberate and intention are key, because they define the difference between actively practicing something and passively learning it.
paragraphs.zh: 归根结底在于学习与练习的区别。托马斯·斯特纳在《练习的心态》一书中阐述了练习与学习的关键区别。他说：“当我们练习某事时，我们是在有意识地重复某个过程，以达到特定目标。”这里的“有意识地”和“意图”是关键，因为它们定义了主动练习与被动学习之间的区别。

paragraphs.en: Learning something new and practicing something new may seem very similar, but these two methods can have profoundly different results. Here are some additional ways to think about the difference. Let's say your goal is to get stronger and more fit. You can research the best instructions on weight lifting technique, but the only way to build strength is to practice lifting weights. Let's say your goal is to grow your startup. You can learn about the best way to make a sales pitch, but the only way to actually land customers is to practice making sales calls.
paragraphs.zh: 学习新东西和练习新东西看似相似，但这两种方法可能产生截然不同的结果。以下是一些理解差异的角度。假设你的目标是变得更强壮、更健康。你可以研究举重技术的最佳指导，但增强力量的唯一方法是练习举重。假设你的目标是发展初创公司。你可以学习推销的最佳方法，但真正赢得客户的唯一方法是练习打销售电话。

paragraphs.en: Let's say your goal is to write a book. You can talk to a best-selling author about writing, but the only way to become a better writer is to practice publishing consistently. Passive learning creates knowledge. Active practice creates skill. Let's consider a couple more reasons to prioritize active practice over passive learning. First, passive learning can be a stick that supports inaction. In many cases, learning is actually a way to avoid taking action on the goals and interests that we say are important to us.
paragraphs.zh: 假设你的目标是写一本书。你可以与畅销书作家谈论写作，但成为更好作家的唯一方法是持续练习发表作品。被动学习创造知识，主动练习创造技能。让我们再考虑几个优先选择主动练习而非被动学习的理由。首先，被动学习可能成为支持不作为的拐杖。在许多情况下，学习实际上是一种逃避对我们口口声声说重要的目标和兴趣采取行动的方式。

paragraphs.en: For example, let's say you want to learn a foreign language. Reading a book on how to learn a foreign language quickly allows you to feel like you are making progress. Of course, you're not actually practicing the action that would deliver your desired outcome (speaking the foreign language). In situations like this one, we often claim that we are preparing or researching the best method, but these rationalizations allow us to feel like we are moving forward when we are merely spinning our wheels. We make the mistake of being in motion rather than taking action. Learning is valuable until it becomes a form of procrastination.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，假设你想学一门外语。阅读一本关于如何快速学习外语的书会让你感觉自己正在进步。当然，你并未真正练习能带来预期结果（说外语）的行动。在这种情况下，我们常声称自己在准备或研究最佳方法，但这些合理化让我们感觉在前进，实则原地踏步。我们犯了“动而不行”的错误。学习是有价值的，直到它沦为一种拖延。

words.meaning: n. 合理化解释，找借口

paragraphs.en: Another point to consider is that practice is learning, but learning is not practice. Passive learning is not a form of practice because although you gain new knowledge, you are not discovering how to apply that knowledge. Active practice, meanwhile, is one of the greatest forms of learning because the mistakes you make while practicing reveal important insights. Even more importantly, practice is the only way to make a meaningful contribution with your knowledge. You can watch an online course about how to build a business or read an article about a terrible disaster in a developing nation, but that knowledge is unproductive unless you actually launch your business or donate to those in need.
paragraphs.zh: 另一点需要考虑的是，练习即学习，但学习不等于练习。被动学习不是一种练习形式，因为尽管你获得了新知识，却未发现如何应用它。而主动练习是最佳的学习形式之一，因为练习中犯的错误能揭示重要洞见。更重要的是，练习是利用知识做出有意义贡献的唯一途径。你可以观看关于如何创业的在线课程，或阅读关于发展中国家惨烈灾难的文章，但除非你真的创业或向需要帮助的人捐款，否则那些知识毫无产出。

paragraphs.en: Learning by itself can be valuable for you, but if you want to be valuable to others, then you have to express your knowledge in some way. The last important point to consider is that practice focuses your energy on the process. Thomas Sterner once said, 'Progress is a natural result of staying focused on the process of doing anything.' The state of your life right now is a result of the habits and beliefs that you have been practicing each day. When you realize this and begin to direct your focus toward practicing better habits day-in and day-out, continual progress will be the logical outcome.
paragraphs.zh: 学习本身对你可能有价值，但若想对他人有价值，就必须以某种方式表达你的知识。最后一点重要考虑是，练习将你的精力集中在过程上。托马斯·斯特纳曾说过：“进步是专注于做任何事情的过程的自然结果。”你当前的生活状态是你每天练习的习惯和信念的结果。当你意识到这一点，并开始将注意力转向日复一日练习更好的习惯时，持续进步将成为合乎逻辑的结果。

paragraphs.en: It is not the things we learn nor the dreams we visualize that determine our results, but rather the habits that we practice each day. Fall in love with boredom and focus your energy on the process, not the product. What does this all mean? Is passive learning useless? Of course not. In many cases, learning for the sake of learning can be a beautiful thing. Not to mention that soaking up new information can help you make more informed decisions when you do decide to take action.
paragraphs.zh: 决定我们结果的并非我们学到的东西或想象的梦想，而是我们每天练习的习惯。爱上枯燥，将精力聚焦于过程而非结果。这一切意味着什么？被动学习毫无用处吗？当然不是。在许多情况下，为学习而学习可以是一件美妙的事。更不用说吸收新信息能帮助你在决定采取行动时做出更明智的决策。

paragraphs.en: That said, the main point of this article is that learning by itself does not lead to progress. We often hide behind information and use learning as an excuse to delay the more difficult and more important choice of actually doing something. Spend less time passively learning and more time actively practicing. Stop thinking and start doing.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，本文的核心观点是，学习本身并不会带来进步。我们常躲在信息背后，以学习为借口推迟那个更困难、更重要的选择——真正去做事。少花时间被动学习，多花时间主动练习。停止空想，立刻行动。
